text:
The athletes will get to keep one of the locks. The other will have a different home.

The world's best athletes arearriving in Parisahead ofFriday's Opening Ceremony-- and they're getting used to their sleeping arrangements.

It's not exactly glamorous, but they're (mostly) finding out that cardboard beds aren't so bad after all.

Watch NBC Bay Area News free wherever you are

As part of efforts to make the2024 Paris Olympicsthe "most responsible and sustainable Games in history," the Olympic Village isfull of cardboard bedsfor athletes, made out of fully recycled materials.

British diver Tom Daley shared a video review of the beds to his 3.2 million followers on Instagram. In the 30-second clip, Daley knocks on the cardboard to show its sturdiness and then jumps up and down on his Paris 2024 comforter without a problem.

A post shared by Tom Daley (@tomdaley)

Irish rugby players Jordan Conroy and Harry McNulty further proved how stable the cardboard beds are with a hilarious Instagram video. McNulty begins the clip by saying the beds will "supposedly" collapse if there's more than one person on it. Then, Conroy dives onto the bed and falls off before jumping back on.

A post shared by Harry McNulty OLY (@mcnuts_)

Australian athlete Tilly Kearns had a different experience for her first night in Paris. The water polo star said the mattress was "actually rock solid," even on the side that was supposed to be softer. She latershared an update on Tuesdaythat her team manager got mattress toppers and extra pillows for the entire Australian team.

A post shared by TK (@tillykearns)

More and more stories will undoubtedly come out as Olympians arrive in Paris, but you can take a full look at the beds thanks to the Olympicson YouTube.
title: Olympic athletes share footage of cardboard beds – NBC Bay Area
description: Olympians from all over the world are arriving in Paris and one of the first things they are doing is examining their unique sleeping arrangements.
